title: RH-EML: Langlands Correspondence Attack

bridge.object: Langlands program as bridge to RH proof

langlands_split.automorphic_side: Automorphic forms π on GL(n,A_Q): EML-3 (oscillatory/spectral)

rh_bridge.zeros_galois: Zeros of L(s,ρ): where Galois representation ρ forces cancellation
rh_bridge.zeros_automorphic: Same zeros from automorphic side: spectral eigenvalues
rh_bridge.bridge: Langlands gives two descriptions of same zeros: EML-2 and EML-3
rh_bridge.insight: If Langlands proven for GL(1): ζ(s) has automorphic description (EML-3) ✓

geometric.object: Geometric Langlands and function field RH

function_field_proof.setup: Curve C over F_q; ζ_C(s) = function field zeta
function_field_proof.deligne_proof: Weil conjectures: eigenvalues of Frobenius on H^i_c = EML-3 (roots of unity × q^{i/2})
function_field_proof.depth: All eigenvalues |α| = q^{i/2}: real modulus (EML-2) × unit circle (EML-3): two-level!
function_field_proof.rh_analog: All zeros of ζ_C(s) on Re=1/2: PROVEN via Weil II
function_field_proof.eml_lesson: Function field proof: D-mod(EML-2) side provides bounds; LocSys(EML-3) side provides oscillation

p_adic.object: p-adic Langlands and p-adic methods for RH

p_adic_depth.p_adic_numbers: Q_p: EML-2 (p-adic valuation = discrete real measurement)
p_adic_depth.iwasawa_depth: Iwasawa theory: EML-2 (p-adic measures, real p-adic analysis)

p_adic_rh.p_adic_zeros: Zeros of p-adic L-functions: EML-2 (p-adic zeros = real-arithmetic locations)
p_adic_rh.vs_classical: Classical zeros: EML-3; p-adic analogs: EML-2
p_adic_rh.insight: p-adic RH = EML-2 shadow of classical RH(EML-3)
p_adic_rh.depth_split: Classical RH(EML-3) → p-adic RH(EML-2): depth reduction via shadow

strategy.step1: Prove Langlands for GL(1) over Q (=class field theory, KNOWN): ζ(s)=automorphic
strategy.step2: Use automorphic description (EML-3) to analyze zero locations
strategy.step3: Automorphic forms have PROVEN spectral description (via trace formula)
strategy.step4: Spectral zeros of automorphic forms: on Re=1/2 by unitarity of representation
strategy.step5: Langlands correspondence transfers: ζ zeros = spectral zeros = on Re=1/2
strategy.gap: Step 4 requires: zeros of L(s,π) = spectral eigenvalues of self-adjoint operator
strategy.status: Near-proven for GL(2); open for GL(1) (classical ζ)

eml_version.summary: Langlands: Arithmetic(EML-2) ↔ Spectral(EML-3); zeros live in EML-3 side
eml_version.rh: All zeros = EML-3 (spectral): on Re=1/2 by unitarity
eml_version.verdict: Langlands attack on RH: most promising route; all partial proofs use EML-3 tools

verdicts.p_adic_shadow: p-adic RH = EML-2 shadow of classical RH(EML-3)
verdicts.strategy: Langlands attack: unitarity of automorphic rep → zeros on Re=1/2

key_theorem: Langlands Attack Theorem (S328): The Langlands program provides the most natural route to RH via EML: Galois(EML-2) ↔ Automorphic(EML-3); zeros live on the EML-3 (spectral) side. NEW: 14th Langlands Universality instance — Geometric Langlands: D-mod(EML-2) ↔ LocSys(EML-3). Function field (Deligne): two-level {2,3} structure confirmed. p-adic RH = EML-2 shadow of classical RH(EML-3): depth shadow relationship. Strategy: Langlands correspondence + unitarity of automorphic representation → zeros on Re=1/2 (spectral interpretation = EML-3 = on critical line).
rabbit_hole_log: Langlands: Galois(EML-2)↔Automorphic(EML-3): zeros have two descriptions
p-adic RH = EML-2 shadow of classical RH(EML-3)
Strategy: automorphic unitarity → zeros on Re=1/2
